verdict.answer_first: B leads with the usable bottom line: "CAP only forces a choice during a network partition" and "real choice is C vs. A"; A explains well but starts less sharply with "When a network failure splits..."
verdict.decisiveness: A is slightly more decisive because it gives one operating rule: "Ask one question" and maps answers to CP/AP; B is strong but spreads the prescription across 3 numbered checks plus caveats.
verdict.fabrication: A invents an unsupported number: "99%+ of the time"; B has no concrete invented number, only the vague claim "partitions are rare."
verdict.actionability: A is easier to use immediately because the table says "Money, inventory, bookings" => CP and "Likes, view counts, feeds" => AP.
